title: rubus fruticosus subsp. plicatus
intro: Rubus fruticosus subsp. plicatus, also known as the double-flowered blackberry, is a subspecies of the blackberry plant native to Europe and Western Asia. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating wounds and inflammation, due to the presence of salicylic acid and other bioactive compounds. The plant is also rich in antioxidants and has been found to have various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help to protect against chronic diseases and support immune function. Rubus fruticosus subsp. plicatus can be prepared as a herbal tea, tincture, or salve, and is often used in traditional medicine to promote wound healing and soothe irritated skin.
